channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: I built Open Source Governance for AI Coding Agents in 2 months. Zero cloud. Zero compromise.
item.description: I built Open Source Governance for AI Coding Agents in 2 months. Zero cloud. Zero compromise. Open Source Governance for AI Coding Agents The Problem The rapid proliferation of AI-assisted...

item.title: Why Doesn't an E-Commerce Payment API Get Called Twice When Users Double-Click the Pay Button?
item.description: Introduction Imagine you're purchasing a product online. You click the "Pay Now" button, and due to a slow internet connection or impatience, you accidentally click it again. A common question...

item.title: The Stale Feature Flag We Deleted That Turned a Feature Back On
item.description: Someone on our team was cleaning up feature flags. It was good instinct...we had a pile of them, and plenty were clearly dead. One in particular had been turned off for over a year. It looked...

item.title: Spec Debt Doesn't Disappear When You Fix It. It Migrates.
item.description: Preface I want to be upfront about something before we get into it. None of the frameworks in this article is mine. The ideas here come from two people who have been thinking about this stuff way...

item.title: I built a tiny PHP framework for AI-readable business APIs
item.description: I have been building a small PHP framework called NENE2. It is not a Laravel replacement. It is not a full-stack framework. It is not trying to hide everything behind magic. NENE2 is a tiny,...

item.title: Every hash chain company builds on rented land. Regulatory Compliance Mapping in Cryptographic Ledgers doesn't.
item.description: Every hash chain company builds on rented land. Regulatory Compliance Mapping in Cryptographic Ledgers doesn't. Regulatory Compliance Mapping in Cryptographic Ledgers: Embedding SOC2 Through UAE...

item.title: Who Actually Owns This Service Account?
item.description: When that AWS service account gets compromised, who do you call? A question that shouldn't be hard. If you're in security or platform engineering, you already know the answer is usually "I have no...

item.title: Every browser engine company builds on rented land. Anti-Enshittification doesn't.
item.description: Every browser engine company builds on rented land. Anti-Enshittification doesn't. Anti-Enshittification: AI-Powered Browser Defenses Against Dark Patterns and Manipulative UI The Problem The...

item.title: Semantic caching our flaky-test summariser: 58% fewer LLM calls
item.description: TL;DR: Our internal flaky-test summariser at Buildkite was firing ~40k LLM calls a day, and most were near-duplicates of failures we'd already explained. Switching on semantic caching in Bifrost...

item.title: What Kind of AI-Assisted Developer Are You? Take the quiz.
item.description: AI makes us faster, but does it make us better engineers, or just more dependent? As a follow-up to my post on the The 4 Cognitive Archetypes of Developers Using AI, I wanted to created a quiz as...

item.title: New OXLOADER Loader Uses Malicious Google Ads to Deliver CastleStealer
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new campaign that delivers CastleStealer by means of a previously unreported malware loader dubbed OXLOADER. According to Elastic Security...

item.title: PP-OCRv6 on Hugging Face: 50-Language OCR from 1.5M to 34.5M Parameters
Wyze’s new smart scale can break down your body composition for less than $80
item.description: Seven months after introducing its $119.98 Ultra BodyScan smart scale, Wyze announced a cheaper $79.98 alternative available today that makes a few compromises to shave $40 off the price. There's...

item.title: Google Sets Sept. 30 Deadline for Android Developer Verification in Four Countries
item.description: Google has set September 30, 2026, as the day it begins enforcing Android developer verification in the first four countries, and the major device-maker app stores are in from the start. On that...

item.title: The Download: record-breaking subsea tunnels and flexible data centers
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Inside the world’s deepest and longest subsea road tunnel...

item.title: Stop Your Legacy Infrastructure from Hijacking Your AI Agents
item.description: Earlier this month, I spoke at the Gartner Security & Risk Management Summit about a blind spot most security programs are still not accounting for - how attackers are circumventing AI security...

item.title: Professional Athletes and Wearables
item.description: I haven’t thought about the privacy issues surrounding professional athletes and wearables. Wearables present serious privacy issues for “Average Joe” consumers, who are entrusting tech companies...

item.title: Read this before you vibe-code another app
item.description: Bob Starr was delighted with his vibe-coded website. "Boomberg" showed how much US tax money is going to tech companies, and Starr launched it online immediately after making it. It wasn't until...

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Browser Bugs, EDR Killers, TV Botnet, OpenBSD Flaw, Android Trojan, and More
item.description: It’s Monday again. This week’s threat list looks painfully familiar: abused integrations, fake tools, poisoned websites, ransomware crews trying to shut down security tools, and mobile malware...

item.title: Some Electricians Think Building Data Centers Is for Sellouts
item.description: Big Tech is throwing big money into data center buildouts. As national opposition to the facilities grows, some workers are beginning to question whether it’s worth it.

item.title: World Cup Scams Are Getting Harder to Spot
item.description: From fake tickets to cloned websites, AI is magnifying World Cup scams. Can fans distinguish between what’s real and what’s not?

item.title: Canada’s Spy Agency Used First-of-Its-Kind Warrant to Clean Botnet-Infected Devices
item.description: Canada's spy service got a judge's permission to reach into infected servers, home routers, and IoT gear sitting on Canadian soil and neutralize two foreign-run botnets. The Federal Court released...

item.title: Inside the world’s deepest and longest subsea road tunnel
item.description: It’s cold, it’s very, very noisy, and—if I can be quite honest with you—I’m not feeling super relaxed. I’m currently around 300 meters, or 1,000 feet, beneath the North Sea, in a dark, dank cave....

item.title: AryStinger Malware Infects 4,300 Legacy Routers to Build Reconnaissance Proxy Network
item.description: A new malware family is turning forgotten home routers into a distributed reconnaissance and proxy network, not the DDoS botnet these devices usually end up in. QiAnXin's XLab calls it AryStinger...

item.title: INTERPOL Warns Phishing, Ransomware, and AI Scams Are Rising Across Asia-Pacific
item.description: A new report from INTERPOL has revealed a "dramatic increase" in cybercrime in Asia and the South Pacific, fueled by rapid digitalization, internet penetration, new technologies, organized...

item.title: Samsung Electronics brings ChatGPT and Codex to employees
item.description: Samsung Electronics deploys ChatGPT Enterprise and Codex to employees worldwide, marking one of OpenAI’s largest enterprise AI rollouts.

item.title: Bose thinks it can be a media company for some reason
item.description: The history books are littered with the corpses of corporate record labels started by companies that had no business being in the music industry. Bose thinks it can be the exception to the rule....

item.title: Cold Court’s debut EP is an infectious, glitchy genre mashup
item.description: Cold Court is a brother-sister duo from Philly that seems to love nothing more than shoving all of their influences together in a messy soup that at least superficially resembles the hyperpop...

item.title: When the Trump administration cracks down on Anthropic, who benefits?
item.description: On the new episode of Equity, we discussed what actually prompted the administration's latest moves against Anthropic, and what this might mean for the AI ecosystem.

item.title: Beyond Siri: Here are the practical AI features coming to your iPhone in iOS 27
item.description: Siri’s AI overhaul may have grabbed the headlines at WWDC, but some of Apple’s most useful AI features are arriving elsewhere in iOS 27.
